text: We address the task of named entity recognition and propose a novel transformer-based architecture called NERFormer. Our model achieves an F1 score of 93.2 on the CoNLL-2003 dataset, surpassing previous state-of-the-art methods by 1.4 points.

This paper tackles machine translation for low-resource language pairs. We introduce a cross-lingual transfer learning approach that leverages multilingual BERT to improve BLEU scores by 5.3 points on the FLORES benchmark for English-Nepali translation.

For the task of sentiment analysis, we fine-tune RoBERTa-large with a domain-adaptive pre-training step on Amazon product reviews. The resulting model obtains 96.1% accuracy on the SST-2 benchmark, establishing a new state-of-the-art result.

We present a novel approach to abstractive summarization using a copy-enhanced sequence-to-sequence model with coverage mechanism. Experiments on the CNN/DailyMail dataset show our method achieves a ROUGE-L score of 41.8, outperforming the baseline by 2.1 points.

Our work focuses on the problem of relation extraction from biomedical text. We propose BioRelEx, a graph neural network model that achieves 87.4 F1 on the ChemProt dataset for chemical-protein interaction extraction.

We tackle question answering in the open-domain setting using a retriever-reader pipeline. Our dense passage retriever combined with an ELECTRA-based reader achieves an exact match score of 42.5 on the Natural Questions benchmark.

This paper addresses coreference resolution in conversational text. We extend the c2f-coref model with dialogue-specific features and evaluate on the LIGHT dataset, achieving a CoNLL F1 of 78.3, a 3.7 point improvement over the baseline.

We investigate the task of aspect-based sentiment analysis using a multi-task learning framework. Our joint model for aspect extraction and sentiment classification achieves 72.1 F1 on the SemEval-2014 Task 4 restaurant review dataset.

For the problem of textual entailment, we propose a decomposable attention model augmented with pre-trained ELMo embeddings. The approach achieves 89.7% accuracy on the SNLI dataset and 78.2% on MultiNLI, demonstrating strong cross-domain generalization.

We study the task of semantic role labeling and introduce a syntax-aware transformer encoder. Our model achieves 87.9 F1 on the CoNLL-2012 benchmark using predicted syntax trees, without requiring gold parse annotations at inference time.
